label: Holonomy Parameter k_gimel
mathematical_goal: Derive the holonomy warp factor k_gimel = b3/2 + 1/pi from G2 geometry
theoretical_significance: The holonomy parameter k_gimel = 12.31830988618379... characterizes the warp factor of the G2 compactification. Its derivation k_gimel = b3/2 + 1/pi = 12 + 0.31831... combines the discrete topological contribution (b3/2 = 12) with a continuous geometric correction (1/pi). This parameter appears in: (1) Neutrino mass sum: Sum(m_nu) = k_gimel/(2*pi*b3), (2) Fine structure constant: alpha^-1 = k_gimel^2 - b3/phi + phi/(4*pi), (3) Higgs VEV: v_tree = k_gimel * (b3-4), (4) CMB temperature: T_tree = phi * k_gimel / (2*pi+1).
edof_note: k_gimel is DERIVED from Pillar Seed b3=24 and mathematical constant pi. It is not an independent input in the EDOF=3 framework.
